title: Profiler, Test Runner, Build Pipeline
summary: Three adjacent editor subsystems wrapped by Mosaic's profiler/*, test/*, and build/* tool families. The Profiler captures per-module CPU / GPU / memory / rendering / physics / audio / UI samples against the editor, a local standalone player, or a remote device, and exposes a scripted read path through ProfilerRecorder plus custom scopes via Profiler.BeginSample. The Test Framework package (com.unity.test-framework) runs NUnit-flavored EditMode and PlayMode tests through the Test Runner window or the -runTests CLI flag. The Build Pipeline compiles player content through BuildPipeline.BuildPlayer, with BuildOptions flags for development/profiler/debug builds and IPreprocessBuildWithReport / IPostprocessBuildWithReport callbacks for per-target fixups. Read this before any tool that records a sample, runs a test, or produces a player.

llmGuidance.when_asked_to_capture_a_specific_stat_at: Use ProfilerRecorder.StartNew(category, statName, capacity). This works in Editor AND built players, unlike the UI Profiler which is editor-only. Hold the Recorder in a field, Dispose in OnDestroy. Blank / zero values usually mean the stat name is wrong or the module isn't enabled on the current platform — double-check ProfilerCategory against the module (Render, Memory, Audio, Physics, Scripts).
llmGuidance.when_wrapping_user_code_for_profiling: Profiler.BeginSample("mosaic.<domain>.<op>") / Profiler.EndSample(). Always pair in try/finally so an exception doesn't leak an open sample. Use a stable, namespaced name — the Profiler groups by exact string, so ad-hoc names fragment the timeline.
llmGuidance.when_choosing_editmode_vs_playmode: Pure logic, editor extensions, or asset-import tests → EditMode (fast, runs on domain reload, no scene load). Anything that needs a running MonoBehaviour, physics step, coroutine timing, or Time.deltaTime progression → PlayMode. Long-running soak and determinism tests always go PlayMode — EditMode has no frame loop.
llmGuidance.when_writing_a_test_that_yields: Use [UnityTest] returning IEnumerator and yield return null / new WaitForSeconds. [Test] methods must be synchronous and void (or return Task in Test Framework 2.x). Mixing them silently disables the test or makes it pass instantly.
llmGuidance.when_running_tests_from_ci:
unity -batchmode -nographics -projectPath

-runTests -testPlatform {EditMode|PlayMode} -testResults -logFile -. Never rely on the Test Runner window — CI must drive BuildPipeline / TestRunnerApi headlessly. Exit code 0 = all passed, 2 = failures, 3 = run error.
llmGuidance.when_a_test_passes_locally_but_fails_in_: First suspects: different asset-import state (library cache not shared), different domain-reload timing, or a PlayMode test depending on an editor-only scene not in EditorBuildSettings.scenes. Re-run with -logFile - and inspect the first compilation error — most CI-only failures are actually build or import errors manifesting as test failure.
llmGuidance.when_producing_a_player_build: Always BuildPipeline.BuildPlayer(new BuildPlayerOptions{...}) from a C# entry point, never the Build menu. Set scenes = EditorBuildSettings.scenes.Where(s => s.enabled).Select(s => s.path). Return BuildReport — summary.result != Succeeded must fail the calling tool, not be logged and ignored.
llmGuidance.when_adding_a_build_side_effect: Implement IPreprocessBuildWithReport for setup (stamp version, bake config) and IPostprocessBuildWithReport for cleanup (strip symbols, sign binary). Order matters — callbackOrder controls execution sequence; duplicate orders run in undefined order. Throwing BuildFailedException aborts the build cleanly.
llmGuidance.when_the_build_fails_on_il2cpp: The most common cause is System.Reflection.Emit usage (dynamic IL generation) which IL2CPP cannot AOT-compile. Audit plugin DLLs and serializers. Unsupported APIs also include most runtime CodeDom. Fix by replacing with source-generated code or a Mono-only assembly guarded by UNITY_IL2CPP.
llmGuidance.when_addressables_are_in_play: AddressableAssetSettings.BuildPlayerContent() is a SEPARATE build step and must run BEFORE BuildPipeline.BuildPlayer if you ship with local-hosted bundles, or independently when shipping remote. Build order drift between player and content causes catalog hash mismatch and runtime 'asset not found' errors. Re-run Addressables build any time a group or entry changes.

moduleMap.CPU Usage: Per-frame main-thread + job-thread sample tree, rooted at PlayerLoop. Click a frame to see the hierarchy.
moduleMap.GPU Usage: Per-frame GPU timings. Requires a compatible graphics API (DX11/12, Vulkan, Metal). Unavailable on some WebGL/older mobile configs.
moduleMap.Rendering: Draw calls, batches, SetPass calls, triangles, vertices. First stop for 'why is my scene slow to render'.
moduleMap.Memory: Total Reserved, Used, GC Allocated In Frame, System Memory. Detailed Mode snapshots need the Memory Profiler package for object-level attribution.
moduleMap.Physics: Active rigidbodies, contacts, queries, solver iterations (PhysX 3D). Physics 2D tracked separately.
moduleMap.Audio: Active voices, DSP CPU, stream count, memory.
moduleMap.UI / UI Details: UGUI rebuild counts, batch counts, layout rebuilds. Cross-reference with Rendering batches.
moduleMap.Global Illumination / Video / VR: Present only when the corresponding subsystem is active.

profiler.attachTargets: Editor (default, in-process) | Local standalone player (auto-discovered if built with Autoconnect Profiler) | Remote device (IP + port, requires Development Build + Autoconnect Profiler). Only one profiler connection at a time.

profilerRecorderApi.notes: Queries most stats exposed in the Profiler UI by name. Read r.LastValue or iterate r.GetSample(i). Capacity is ring-buffer length. Stat names are case-sensitive and platform-specific; enumerate with ProfilerRecorderHandle.GetAvailable to discover what's live on the current build.

profiler.frameDebugger: Separate tool (Window → Analysis → Frame Debugger). Pauses rendering mid-frame and lets you step through every draw call with the full render state. Not part of the Profiler and not scriptable — UI-driven investigation only.

testFramework.package: com.unity.test-framework (built into 2021.3+). Adds Test Runner window under Window → General → Test Runner.

modes.EditMode: Runs on a domain reload in the editor process. No scene is loaded. No frame loop. Fast. Use for pure logic, asset-import tests, editor tooling tests.
modes.PlayMode: Enters Play mode with a minimal scene (or a specified one). Frame loop runs, MonoBehaviour lifecycle fires, coroutines work. Use for anything requiring runtime behavior.

nunitConventions.[TestFixture]: Optional on the class — inferred when the class contains [Test] methods.
nunitConventions.[SetUp] / [TearDown]: Run before / after each test method.
nunitConventions.[Test]: Synchronous test. Must return void (or Task in TF 2.x).
nunitConventions.[UnityTest]: Coroutine-style test. Must return IEnumerator. Yield null to advance a frame; yield a WaitForSeconds to advance time. EditMode UnityTest runs via editor update loop; PlayMode UnityTest runs via MonoBehaviour coroutine.
nunitConventions.[Category(\"slow\")]: Filter flag used by -testCategory on the CLI and the filter box in the UI.

cliInvocation.exitCodes: 0 = all green, 2 = failures, 3 = run error (compile failure, missing dependencies).
cliInvocation.resultsFormat: NUnit3 XML. CI systems (GitHub Actions, TeamCity, Jenkins) have native renderers.

testFramework.assemblyWiring: Tests live in a separate assembly defined by an .asmdef with "includePlatforms": [] and "defineConstraints": ["UNITY_INCLUDE_TESTS"]. It must reference the assembly under test plus nunit.framework and UnityEngine.TestRunner / UnityEditor.TestRunner. Without the constraint, tests ship with the player.

buildPipeline.entryPoint: BuildPipeline.BuildPlayer(BuildPlayerOptions options) → BuildReport. Inspect report.summary.result (Succeeded / Failed / Cancelled / Unknown) and report.summary.totalErrors.

buildPlayerOptions.scenes: string[] of project-relative scene paths. Conventionally sourced from EditorBuildSettings.scenes.Where(s => s.enabled).
buildPlayerOptions.locationPathName: Output path. For iOS/Android this is a directory; for Windows/Mac/Linux a file path with appropriate extension.
buildPlayerOptions.target: BuildTarget enum (StandaloneWindows64, StandaloneOSX, Android, iOS, WebGL, PS5, XboxSeries, LinuxServer, ...).
buildPlayerOptions.targetGroup: BuildTargetGroup enum — some APIs (player settings) key off group rather than target.

buildOptionsFlags.Development: Enables the debug console, development-mode asserts, and the Autoconnect / profiler port.
buildOptionsFlags.ConnectWithProfiler: Auto-attach the Profiler on player start. Requires Development.
buildOptionsFlags.AllowDebugging: Enables managed-script debugger attach. Requires Development.
buildOptionsFlags.EnableDeepProfilingSupport: Instruments every managed method boundary. Heavy overhead — use only for targeted investigation.
buildOptionsFlags.BuildScriptsOnly: Recompile managed scripts against the last build artifacts; fastest iteration on a code-only change.
buildOptionsFlags.CleanBuildCache: Invalidate the incremental build cache. Use when hunting a 'works on clean build' bug.
buildOptionsFlags.IncludeTestAssemblies: Ship test assemblies in the player. Almost always wrong for a release build.

callbacks.IPreprocessBuildWithReport.OnPreprocessB: Fires before the player build begins. Good for version stamping, config baking, verifying environment.
callbacks.IPostprocessBuildWithReport.OnPostproces: Fires after the player is written. Good for signing, symbol stripping, artifact upload.
callbacks.IProcessSceneWithReport.OnProcessScene(S: Fires per scene mid-build. Rare; used for scene-level optimization passes.
callbacks.ordering: All implement callbackOrder. Lower runs first. Duplicate orders are undefined — assign deliberate, spaced values (100, 200, 300).

addressablesBuild.sequencing: Must run BEFORE BuildPipeline.BuildPlayer when shipping bundles inside the player, or independently when shipping remote. A player build that used a newer Addressables catalog than was baked in will runtime-fail with catalog mismatch.
addressablesBuild.rebuildTriggers: Any change to an AddressableAssetGroup, entry address, label set, or schema version invalidates the catalog. Re-run BuildPlayerContent.

decisionRules.rule: PlayMode + [UnityTest] + ProfilerRecorder on CPU frame time.
decisionRules.evidence: EditMode has no frame loop; a UnityTest in EditMode runs via editor updates and does not exercise MonoBehaviour lifecycle.
Fastest feedback loop; no scene / domain cost.

decisionRules.scenario: CI build of a player for any target
decisionRules.evidence: Menu-driven builds don't have a scripted failure-signal path; CI needs BuildReport.summary.result.

decisionRules.scenario: Need to sample a stat in a shipped player
decisionRules.evidence: The Profiler UI is editor-only; ProfilerRecorder is the only supported runtime read path.

decisionRules.scenario: Identifying which managed method spiked this frame
decisionRules.rule: Development + EnableDeepProfilingSupport build, then Profiler hierarchy view.
decisionRules.evidence: Deep profiling is the only way to see methods below the instrumented sample boundaries, at the cost of substantial overhead.

commonPitfalls.cause: Stat name misspelled, stat not available on current platform, or category mismatch.
commonPitfalls.fix: Enumerate available handles with ProfilerRecorderHandle.GetAvailable and log the exact names the runtime exposes.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Tests pass in the editor, fail in CI with 'type or namespace not found'
commonPitfalls.cause: Test asmdef missing UNITY_INCLUDE_TESTS define constraint or not referencing UnityEditor.TestRunner for EditMode tests.
commonPitfalls.fix: Fix the asmdef; run once locally in batchmode to reproduce before pushing.

commonPitfalls.symptom: IL2CPP build fails with 'Reflection.Emit is not supported'
commonPitfalls.cause: A dependency generates IL at runtime (common in older serializers, some expression-tree libraries).
commonPitfalls.fix: Replace with a source-generator equivalent, or isolate the dependency behind #if !ENABLE_IL2CPP with a Mono-only fallback path.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Player launches but Addressables throw 'Exception: RemoteProviderException'
commonPitfalls.cause: Addressables content was not rebuilt after the player build reordered / renamed a group.
commonPitfalls.fix: Always AddressableAssetSettings.BuildPlayerContent() before BuildPipeline.BuildPlayer in the same script; treat them as one logical step.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Custom Profiler.BeginSample scope never appears in the hierarchy
commonPitfalls.cause: EndSample was skipped due to an early return / exception, and Unity swallowed the unmatched open sample.
commonPitfalls.fix: Always pair in try/finally; alternatively use the using-scope ProfilerMarker.Auto().

commonPitfalls.symptom: Build callback runs twice or in the wrong order
commonPitfalls.cause: Two IPreprocessBuildWithReport implementations share the same callbackOrder.
commonPitfalls.fix: Assign unique, deliberately spaced callbackOrder values (100, 200, 300) so insertion order is no longer load-bearing.

versionDeltas.change: ProfilerRecorder / ProfilerRecorderHandle APIs introduced. First supported way to read profiler counters from player code at runtime.
ProfilerMarker struct stabilized as the preferred low-overhead alternative to Profiler.BeginSample/EndSample for hot paths.
Test Framework 1.1.x is the baseline. Build Pipeline IPreprocess/IPostprocessBuildWithReport interfaces are stable across the 2021.3 → 6000.4 range.
IL2CPP gained improved generic sharing and incremental compilation, cutting cold-build times. No API surface change for callers.
Test Framework 2.0 ships. async Task test methods are officially supported; NUnit upgraded to 3.14. Parallel test execution flag added.
versionDeltas.migrationNote: Tests written against TF 1.x continue to work. New async APIs unlock cleaner await-based tests but are optional.

versionDeltas.change: BuildPipeline and Profiler APIs unchanged from 2022.3 LTS. Unity 6 adds GPU Resident Drawer stats exposed through new ProfilerCategory.Render counters.
Incremental IL2CPP enabled by default; BuildOptions.CleanBuildCache more impactful for troubleshooting. No code changes required.

externalReferences.title: Unity Manual — Running tests from the command line

authorshipNotes: Content authored by Mosaic team from publicly-documented Unity APIs and the Unity Manual / ScriptReference pages covering the Profiler, ProfilerRecorder, ProfilerMarker, the Test Framework package (com.unity.test-framework), NUnit attributes, the command-line test runner, BuildPipeline.BuildPlayer, BuildPlayerOptions / BuildOptions, BuildReport, the IPreprocessBuildWithReport / IPostprocessBuildWithReport interfaces, and Addressables build scripts. API signatures, enum values, stat names, callback interface names, and command-line flags are factual and not copyrightable. Explanatory prose, decision rules, llmGuidance scenarios, commonPitfalls, and versionDeltas are original Mosaic editorial content informed by observed LLM tool-call failure modes (missing ProfilerRecorder stat names, unpaired BeginSample, EditMode/PlayMode misuse, ignored BuildReport.summary.result, stale Addressables catalogs, IL2CPP Reflection.Emit crashes). No Unity documentation text is reproduced verbatim; manual and script-reference pages are cited as externalReferences for the LLM to fetch when it needs source text.
